actions: Read spec.md — RQ8 definition: token reduction measurable via prompt-cache.ts + budget-allocator.ts
Read iteration-007.md — prior iter next-focus + findings (F-039 through F-042, DEFER verdict for eval harness to sub-packet 028)
Read iteration-006.md — RQ6 findings (F-038 hook for RQ8, proposed render.ts changes at lines 149-168)
Read external/README.md:185-189 — extract XCE ~20% token reduction claim: 'Our benchmarks show ~20% token reduction when the agent uses XCE proactively' at line 188

Read skill_advisor/lib/prompt-cache.ts (192 lines) — full audit: cache mechanics, ADVISOR_PROMPT_CACHE_DEFAULT_MAX_TOKENS=80 at line 10, ADVISOR_PROMPT_CACHE_MAX_TOKENS=120 at line 11, normalizeMaxTokens() at lines 57-65, cache hit/miss counters at lines 102-117. Verdict: input-side token caps only — ZERO session token measurement

Read code_graph/lib/budget-allocator.ts (134 lines) — full audit: DEFAULT_FLOORS at lines 32-38 (700/1200/900/400/800), allocateBudget() at lines 52-117 (floor→overflow→trim algorithm), AllocationResult.totalUsed at line 112 (sum of granted, not measured). Verdict: pre-allocation planner for 4000-token compact brief — ZERO session token measurement
Read code_graph/handlers/context.ts (416 lines) — handler surface: budgetTokens input at line 46, passes to contextArgs at line 343. Response JSON does NOT include token consumption in output shape

Read code_graph/lib/code-graph-context.ts — ContextResult.metadata.budgetUsed at line 42 (Math.ceil(text.length/4) heuristic at line 199), budgetLimit at line 43, partialOutput at lines 45-51. Verdict: single-call-scoped heuristic — NOT session measurement

Read code_graph/lib/compact-merger.ts — estimateTokens() at lines 53-55 (chars/4 heuristic), truncateToTokens() at lines 58-68 (section-level budget truncation). Verdict: section-level heuristic — NOT measurement

Read code_graph/lib/code-graph-db.ts:1236-1244 — getTokenUsageRatio() consumes external sessionMetrics (estimatedPromptTokens + estimatedCompletionTokens). Computes completion/total ratio from already-counted tokens

Grep mcp_server/**/*.ts for estimatedPromptTokens|estimatedCompletionTokens — 90+ matches: hook-state.ts:227-228 (schema), response-hints.ts:48-53 (extract from API), session-stop.ts:384-385 (persist), session-analytics-db.ts:100-104 (schema columns), 12+ test files

Read lib/analytics/session-analytics-db.ts:90-141 — analytics_sessions table: prompt_tokens (line 100), completion_tokens (line 101), total_tokens (line 104), turn_count (line 110). analytics_turns per-turn breakdown at lines 115-137. getSessionAggregate() at lines 409-428 with COALESCE(SUM(total_tokens))
Read hooks/response-hints.ts:40-65 — extractSurfacedTokenCount() intake point: reads promptTokens/completionTokens/cacheTokens from LLM API response usage envelope (lines 48-64)

Read skill_advisor/lib/render.ts:39-65 — DEFAULT_TOKEN_CAP=80 (line 39), AMBIGUOUS_TOKEN_CAP=120 (line 40), capText() with TOKEN_TO_CHAR_ESTIMATE=4 (lines 59-64) — brief size controls, not session measurement

label: XCE token reduction claim — 'Our benchmarks show ~20% token reduction when the agent uses XCE proactively' (external/README.md:188). Mechanism attribution: 'Without steering, the agent may use XCE sometimes but still fall back to reading files directly. With steering, the agent consistently uses XCE first.' Causal chain: stronger directive → agent uses XCE first → fewer file-read tokens → fewer total tokens. Independent variable is directive strength, not tool implementation. Aligns with RQ6 proposed render.ts change ('use' → 'MUST invoke X FIRST')

evidence.quote: Our benchmarks show ~20% token reduction when the agent uses XCE proactively
Without steering, the agent may use XCE sometimes but still fall back to reading files directly. With steering, the agent consistently uses XCE first
at 16x lower cost

verdict: CITE — claim extracted; methodology black-box, no confidence interval or measurement protocol provided

label: Survey of 4 token-related code layers reveals ZERO per-session token measurement: (1) prompt-cache.ts:10-12 — input-side brief token caps (80 default, 120 max), cache key discriminator only, hit/miss counters at lines 102-117. (2) budget-allocator.ts:52-117 — pre-allocation planner distributing 4000-token budget across 4-5 sources, totalUsed computed as sum of grants (line 112). (3) code-graph-context.ts:42-43 — single-response budgetUsed/budgetLimit via chars/4 heuristic (line 199). (4) compact-merger.ts:53-55 — section-level estimateTokens() heuristic. All are input constraints or single-call-scoped heuristics — ZERO session-level token counting inside the MCP server

evidence.quote: ADVISOR_PROMPT_CACHE_DEFAULT_MAX_TOKENS = 80; ADVISOR_PROMPT_CACHE_MAX_TOKENS = 120
export function allocateBudget(sources, totalBudget = 4000): AllocationResult — floor assignment → overflow redistribution → budget cap trim
budgetUsed: number; budgetLimit: number — in ContextResult.metadata
budgetUsed: Math.ceil(formattedTextBrief.text.length / 4) — 4-char-per-token heuristic
function estimateTokens(text: string): number { return Math.ceil(text.length / 4); }

verdict: GAP — no session-level token measurement in MCP server; measurement MUST use session-analytics-db.ts externally

label: Session-level token counting EXISTS but OUTSIDE the MCP server instrumentation layer — in the session analytics infrastructure: (A) analytics_sessions table (session-analytics-db.ts:100-104): prompt_tokens, completion_tokens, total_tokens, turn_count — all INTEGER, default 0. (B) analytics_turns per-turn breakdown (lines 125-129): same token columns per transcript turn. (C) Hook state schema (hook-state.ts:227-228): estimatedPromptTokens and estimatedCompletionTokens — z.number().finite().nonnegative(). (D) Intake point (response-hints.ts:48-53): extractSurfacedTokenCount() reads promptTokens/completionTokens from LLM API response usage envelope. (E) Session-stop persistence (session-stop.ts:384-385): writes final API token usage into analytics DB. The MCP server RECEIVES token counts from the LLM provider hook API — it does not count them itself

evidence.quote: prompt_tokens INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0, completion_tokens INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0, total_tokens INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0
prompt_tokens INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0, completion_tokens INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0, total_tokens INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0 — analytics_turns per-turn breakdown
estimatedPromptTokens: z.number().finite().nonnegative(), estimatedCompletionTokens: z.number().finite().nonnegative()
extractSurfacedTokenCount reads promptTokens/completionTokens from result.usage, meta.usage — intake point from LLM API response
estimatedPromptTokens: usage.promptTokens, estimatedCompletionTokens: usage.completionTokens — session-stop persistence

verdict: EXTERNAL — token counting is provider-side; MCP server only persists what the hook API reports

label: Measurement protocol for token reduction validation: (1) Independent variable: brief strength — current 'use' (render.ts:155-158) vs proposed 'MUST invoke X FIRST — <hint>' (RQ6 F-036). (2) Dependent variable: session total_tokens from analytics_sessions table (session-analytics-db.ts:104). (3) Protocol: Phase 1 BASELINE — run same task N≥10 times with 'use' brief, query getSessionRow() for total_tokens + turn_count post-session-stop. Phase 2 AFTER — run same task N≥10 times with 'MUST invoke' brief, query same. (4) Comparison: Δ = mean(BASELINE) − mean(AFTER); % reduction = 100 × Δ / mean(BASELINE); Welch's t-test. (5) Instrumentation hook: ~80 LOC in sub-packet 028 — token query helper (~25 LOC), delta compute (~25 LOC), harness integration (~30 LOC). Expected effect: 5-15% reduction (lower than XCE's 20% due to dynamic advisor firing vs unconditional steering, and different tool output format: structural graph vs narrative HLD/LLD)

evidence.quote: capText(`Advisor: ${result.freshness}; use ${topLabel} ${formatScore(top.confidence)}/${formatScore(top.uncertainty)} pass.`, Math.min(tokenCap, DEFAULT_TOKEN_CAP)) — current brief format
getSessionRow() returns SessionAnalyticsSessionRow with total_tokens; getSessionAggregate() with COALESCE(SUM(total_tokens), 0) AS total_tokens
total_tokens INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0 — analytics_sessions column
~20% token reduction when the agent uses XCE proactively — comparison target

verdict: ADAPT — measurement deferrable to sub-packet 028; protocol fully defined; expected 5-15% reduction (lower than XCE 20%)

label: Verdict ADAPT for token-reduction harness. ADOPT rejected: XCE's measurement methodology is black-box (no confidence interval, task set, or protocol in README). DEFER rejected: measurement protocol is fully defined (F-046), depends only on existing session-analytics-db.ts queries, and is the necessary evidence for RQ6 render change justification. SKIP rejected: without RQ8 measurement, RQ6's 30-LOC render change ships without quantified justification — violates research packet's evidence-first principle. Implementation path: sub-packet 028 (eval harness from RQ7 F-042) + sub-packet 029 (token instrumentation ~80 LOC) → baseline run → deploy RQ6 change → after run → publish delta. Risk: effect may be smaller than XCE's 20% (dynamic firing, structural vs narrative output) — expect 5-15%

evidence.quote: ~20% token reduction when the agent uses XCE proactively — XCE's unvalidated claim, no methodology published
prompt_tokens, completion_tokens, total_tokens, turn_count — session token columns available for query
use ${topLabel} — current brief strength, independent variable for baseline-vs-after comparison

verdict: ADAPT — ~80 LOC in sub-packet 029; harness queries session-analytics-db.ts post-session-stop; effect expected 5-15%
